name: Layered config resolution with CLI override precedence
description: Add CLI flag overrides for experiment.json values in codeprobe run. Currently run_cmd.py reads model/agent from CLI flags and experiment.json but there's no layered resolution — CLI flags like --model and --timeout don't override experiment.json config values. Implement: (1) In run_cmd.py, add --timeout option to the run Click command. (2) In _run_config(), when CLI flags are non-None, override the experiment config values: model from --model, timeout from --timeout. (3) Add --repeats option passthrough (currently hardcoded to 1 in CLI but execute_config supports it). (4) Log config resolution at debug level showing final resolved values. (5) Document precedence: built-in defaults < experiment.json < CLI flags. Write tests verifying CLI overrides win.
acceptance: codeprobe run . --model opus-4 overrides experiment.json model value for all configs
codeprobe run . --timeout 600 overrides the default 300s timeout in experiment.json extra.timeout_seconds
codeprobe run . --repeats 3 passes repeats=3 to execute_config
At -v debug level, log lines show 'Config resolution: model=opus-4 (CLI override)' or similar
When --model is not provided, experiment.json value is used (no regression)
New test in tests/test_run_config_resolution.py verifies: CLI flag overrides experiment.json, experiment.json overrides defaults, absent CLI flags fall through
Existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x

name: Adapter lazy imports and output contract tests
description: Ensure missing CLI tools don't crash codeprobe at import time, and add contract tests for adapter output fields. (1) In registry.py, wrap _import_class() calls in try/except ImportError so that importing an adapter module that references a missing optional dependency doesn't crash. The error should surface only at resolve() time with a clear message. (2) In adapters/__init__.py, remove direct imports of adapter classes — the __init__.py currently only re-exports protocol types, which is fine. Verify each adapter (claude, copilot, codex, aider, openai_compat) only imports stdlib + codeprobe modules at module level, deferring optional deps (openai, anthropic) to method bodies or lazy import. (3) Write contract tests: for each adapter, create a fixture AgentOutput with realistic values and assert cost_usd is not None, cost_model is not None, cost_source is not None, input_tokens is not None, output_tokens is not None. These are fixture-based — no live agent calls.
acceptance: When copilot CLI is not installed, 'import codeprobe' does not crash
When copilot CLI is not installed, 'codeprobe run . --agent copilot' produces a clear error message (not an ImportError traceback)
tests/test_adapter_contracts.py exists with contract tests for each adapter's output fixture
Each contract test asserts cost_usd, cost_model, cost_source, input_tokens, output_tokens are not None for a fixture output
Existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x

name: codeprobe preambles list command
description: Add a 'preambles' subcommand group to the CLI with a 'list' command. (1) Create src/codeprobe/cli/preamble_cmd.py with the preambles group and list command. (2) The list command shows available preambles at each search path level: built-in (from codeprobe/preambles/*.md), user (~/.codeprobe/preambles/), project (.codeprobe/preambles/), and task-level. For each preamble, show: name, source level (built-in/user/project), and template variables used (scan for {{var}} patterns). (3) Register the preambles group in cli/__init__.py. (4) Use preambles/__init__.py list_builtins() for built-in discovery. Scan user/project dirs with glob. (5) Write tests.
acceptance: codeprobe preambles list runs without error and shows built-in preambles with their source level
Output includes template variables found in each preamble (e.g., '{{repo_path}}, {{task_id}}')
User-level preambles from ~/.codeprobe/preambles/ are shown if they exist
Project-level preambles from .codeprobe/preambles/ are shown if they exist
src/codeprobe/cli/preamble_cmd.py exists
tests/test_preamble_cmd.py exists and passes
Existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x

name: Prompt dry-run (--show-prompt flag)
description: Add --show-prompt flag to codeprobe run that prints the fully-resolved prompt for the first task without calling any agent. (1) In cli/__init__.py, add --show-prompt flag to the run command. (2) In run_cmd.py's run_eval(), when show_prompt is True: load experiment, resolve first config, load first task's instruction, resolve preambles, compose the full prompt via compose_instruction(), print it to stdout, and exit. No agent is spawned. (3) This is useful for debugging prompt composition — users can see exactly what text the agent receives.
acceptance: codeprobe run . --show-prompt prints the fully-resolved prompt text to stdout and exits without spawning an agent
The output includes instruction text, preamble content (if configured), and repo path context
If preambles are configured in experiment.json, they appear in the output with template variables resolved
Exit code is 0 on success
tests/test_show_prompt.py exists with a test that verifies prompt output includes instruction text
Existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x

name: codeprobe doctor command
description: Add a 'doctor' command that checks environment readiness. (1) Create src/codeprobe/cli/doctor_cmd.py with check functions for: installed agents (shutil.which for claude, copilot, codex, aider), API keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN — check os.environ, don't print values), git status (is current dir a git repo), Python version (>=3.11). (2) For each check, print PASS/FAIL with a fix suggestion on failure. E.g., 'FAIL: claude CLI not found — install with: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code'. (3) Register the command in cli/__init__.py. (4) Write tests using monkeypatch to simulate present/absent tools.
acceptance: codeprobe doctor runs without error
Output shows PASS/FAIL for each check: claude CLI, copilot CLI, codex CLI, aider CLI, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, git repo status, Python version
FAIL entries include a fix suggestion
API key values are never printed (only presence/absence)
Exit code is 0 if all checks pass, 1 if any check fails
src/codeprobe/cli/doctor_cmd.py exists
tests/test_doctor_cmd.py exists and passes
Existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x

name: User-defined mine profiles
description: Extend the mine preset system to allow user-defined profiles stored in config files. (1) Define profile storage: ~/.codeprobe/mine-profiles.json (user-level) and .codeprobe/mine-profiles.json (project-level). Each profile is a JSON object with flag names as keys. (2) Add --profile flag to codeprobe mine that loads a named profile from the config file. Profile values act like presets — explicit CLI flags override them. Precedence: built-in defaults < profile < --preset < explicit CLI flags. (3) Add 'codeprobe mine --save-profile <name>' that saves current flag values as a named profile. (4) Add 'codeprobe mine --list-profiles' that shows available profiles. (5) Write tests.
acceptance: codeprobe mine --save-profile my-setup --count 10 --org-scale saves profile 'my-setup' to ~/.codeprobe/mine-profiles.json
codeprobe mine --profile my-setup /path/to/repo loads saved flags from the profile
codeprobe mine --profile my-setup --count 5 /path/to/repo uses count=5 (explicit flag overrides profile)
codeprobe mine --list-profiles shows available profiles from user and project levels
Project-level profiles in .codeprobe/mine-profiles.json override user-level profiles with the same name
tests/test_mine_profiles.py exists and passes
Existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x

name: Ctrl+C integration test
description: Write an integration test that spawns a real codeprobe subprocess and verifies graceful SIGINT handling. (1) Create tests/test_ctrlc_integration.py. The test creates a minimal experiment with a task that sleeps (e.g., test.sh that does 'sleep 30 && exit 0'). It spawns 'python -m codeprobe run .' as a subprocess, waits 2 seconds, sends SIGINT. (2) Assert: process exits with code 130, partial results are written to disk (checkpoint.db has entries), no Python traceback in stderr. (3) Mark test with @pytest.mark.integration and @pytest.mark.skipif for environments without signal support. (4) Use a tmpdir-based experiment setup with a real git repo (git init + commit).
acceptance: tests/test_ctrlc_integration.py exists
Test spawns a real subprocess running codeprobe run
Test sends SIGINT (signal.SIGINT) after 2 seconds
Test asserts exit code is 130
Test asserts no Python traceback in stderr output
Test asserts partial results exist on disk (checkpoint.db or results directory is non-empty)
Test is marked with @pytest.mark.integration
Test works on Python 3.11+
pytest tests/test_ctrlc_integration.py passes (when run in a git repo context)
